design.title: Dhvani — Resilient Tricolour Project Pitch
design.reference: Apple Keynote dark mode with Indian tricolour accents

slides.title: Cover — Dhvani
slides.task_brief: Cover slide. Large hero title: 'Dhvani' in white with saffron accent on 'Dh' and green on 'vi'. Subtitle: 'Smart & Resilient National Flag System'. Below: 'Resilient Tricolour Project | India Innovates Initiative'. Bottom left: 'Team Hamaraneta'. Tricolour bar at top (6px: saffron, white, green). Bottom right small text: 'Pitch for Hon'ble CM Smt. Rekha Gupta'. Dark background (#0F0F0F). Make it bold and dramatic. Speaker notes: Full 60-second pitch script: 'Namaste. Delhi flies 500 giant national flags with pride — but maintaining them costs 104 crore rupees every year. Storms tear them apart. Pollution degrades them. The government is considering doubling the budget to 208 crores. But we have a better way. Introducing Dhvani — a Smart and Resilient National Flag System that combines advanced material science, IoT sensors, and AI monitoring to extend flag lifespan by 3x and slash costs by 86 percent. Here is how.'

slides.title: The ₹104 Crore Problem
slides.task_brief: Problem slide. Title at top: 'The Problem' in white. Three hero stats in a row with big numbers: '₹104 Cr' (annual cost, in saffron), '4→8x' (proposed replacement doubling, in red/white), '500 Flags' (across Delhi, in green). Below the stats, a row of 3 pain-point cards with icons: (1) Storm Damage — 'Severe summer storms tear flag fabric, requiring emergency replacements', (2) Pollution — 'Delhi's AQI accelerates fabric degradation and discoloration', (3) Manual Inspection — 'No real-time monitoring — damage detected only during physical visits'. Tricolour bar at top. Dark bg. Speaker notes: 'Today, Delhi spends 104 crore rupees annually on 500 high-mast flags — replacing them 4 times a year. Storms tear them. Pollution degrades them. And the proposed solution? Double the replacements to 8 times a year — costing 208 crores. There is no real-time monitoring. Damage is discovered only when someone physically visits the site. This is unsustainable.'

slides.title: Our Solution — 3 Pillars
slides.task_brief: Solution overview slide. Title: 'Dhvani: 3-Pillar Smart Flag System'. Three large pillar cards side by side: (1) Material Innovation — Icon: test tube/microscope. 'Kevlar-Polyester Blend + Nano Coating' — '4x stronger fabric, self-cleaning, 12-month lifespan vs 3 months'. Badge: '3x Lifespan'. (2) IoT + AI Monitoring — Icon: chip/sensor. '5 Sensors Per Mast + AI Computer Vision' — 'Real-time tear detection, auto-alerts in <3 seconds, 94% AI confidence'. Badge: 'Real-time'. (3) Sustainable Lifecycle — Icon: recycle leaf. 'Flag Code Compliant Disposal + Recycling' — '100% respectful disposal with FCC certification, ₹30L/yr recycling revenue'. Badge: 'Compliant'. Each card has saffron/green accent border-left. Tricolour bar at top. Speaker notes: 'Our solution has three pillars. First: Material Innovation — a Kevlar-Polyester blend that is 4 times stronger, with nano-coating that makes it self-cleaning. Lifespan jumps from 3 months to 12 months. Second: IoT and AI — 5 sensors on every mast, connected via LoRaWAN, with AI computer vision that detects tears in under 3 seconds with 94 percent confidence. Third: Sustainable Lifecycle — 100 percent Flag Code compliant disposal with recycling that generates 30 lakh rupees per year.'

slides.title: Material Innovation Deep Dive
slides.task_brief: Material science slide. Title: 'Material Innovation: 3x Stronger, Self-Cleaning'. Left side: Comparison table showing Current (Standard Polyester: 3 months, 450 N/m² tensile, ₹350/m²) vs Proposed (Kevlar-Polyester Blend: 12 months, 1200 N/m² tensile, ₹1450/m²) with green checkmarks on proposed row. Right side: Three feature cards stacked: (1) 'Kevlar Fiber Reinforcement' — '15% Kevlar blend withstands 150 km/h winds', (2) 'TiO2 Nano Coating' — 'UV-activated self-cleaning reduces washing by 70%', (3) 'Graphene (Future)' — '18-month lifespan, ultra-lightweight — ready for Phase 4'. Tricolour bar at top. Speaker notes: 'The current polyester fabric lasts just 3 months. Our Kevlar-Polyester blend lasts 12 months — that is 3 times longer. It withstands wind speeds of 150 kilometers per hour. The TiO2 nano-coating is self-cleaning — UV light breaks down dust and pollutants automatically, reducing washing frequency by 70 percent. And we have a graphene-reinforced fabric in the pipeline that lasts 18 months.'

slides.title: IoT + AI Monitoring
slides.task_brief: IoT slide. Title: 'Smart Monitoring: Detect Damage in <3 Seconds'. Left half: 5 sensor icons in a vertical list with labels: Accelerometer (vibration patterns), Strain Gauge (fabric tension), Wind Speed (auto-retract >80 km/h), Humidity (moisture absorption), Camera AI-CV (YOLOv8 tear detection). Bottom of left: Communication stack badges: 'LoRaWAN 868MHz' → '4G LTE' → 'Edge TPU AI' → '<3s Alert'. Right half: Large diagram showing 4-step AI pipeline vertically: (1) CAPTURE — '360° camera every 30 seconds', (2) DETECT — 'YOLOv8 on Edge TPU, 94% mAP', (3) CLASSIFY — 'Minor / Major / Critical severity', (4) ALERT — 'Push to Command Center + auto work order'. Each step numbered 1-4 with saffron accent circles. Tricolour bar at top. Speaker notes: 'Every flag mast gets 5 sensors — accelerometer, strain gauge, wind speed, humidity, and a 360-degree camera with AI. The camera runs YOLOv8 on a Google Edge TPU. It captures every 30 seconds, detects tears with 94 percent accuracy, classifies severity, and pushes an alert to the command center in under 3 seconds. No more waiting for someone to physically visit the site.'

slides.title: ₹61 Cr Savings — Cost & ROI
slides.task_brief: Cost and ROI slide. Title: 'The Result: 86.5% Cost Reduction'. Row of 4 big stat blocks: '₹104 Cr' (current cost, crossed out in red), '₹43 Cr' (with Dhvani, in green), '86.5%' (savings, in saffron), '8.5 mo' (payback period, in white). Below: Investment breakdown card — 'Investment: ₹43 Cr Year 1' with items: Kevlar Fabric ₹31 Cr, IoT Sensors ₹6.5 Cr, AI/CV Platform ₹3.5 Cr, Software + Training ₹2 Cr. Right side: 5-year impact card — '5-Year Cumulative Savings: ₹305 Cr', 'ROI: 7.1x', 'Annual Savings: ₹61 Cr/year'. Tricolour bar at top. Speaker notes: 'The numbers speak for themselves. Current cost: 104 crores. With Dhvani: 43 crores. That is an 86.5 percent reduction — saving 61 crores every single year. The total investment is 43 crores in Year 1, with a payback period of just 8.5 months. Over 5 years, we save 305 crores — a 7.1 times return on investment. And this is with the Kevlar blend. Our graphene roadmap takes savings even higher.'

slides.title: Implementation Roadmap
slides.task_brief: Roadmap slide. Title: '18-Month Implementation Plan'. Horizontal timeline with 4 phases as cards: Phase 1 (Month 1-3, ₹8.5 Cr, saffron accent) — 'Pilot: 25 IoT kits on India Gate, Rashtrapati Bhavan, Parliament. Nano-coating trial on 10 flags.'; Phase 2 (Month 4-8, ₹22 Cr, blue accent) — 'Rollout: IoT on all 500 flags, 8 zone gateways, AI-CV at 100 sites, Kevlar fabric deployment.'; Phase 3 (Month 9-14, ₹15 Cr, green accent) — 'Full Smart System: Auto-retract mechanisms, IMD weather integration, predictive ML, recycling program launch.'; Phase 4 (Month 15-18, ₹5 Cr, purple accent) — 'Scale: Graphene on 50 VIP flags, Delhi 311 integration, expand to Mumbai/Chennai/Kolkata.'. Total investment badge at bottom: '₹43 Cr Total | Payback: 8.5 Months'. Tricolour bar at top. Speaker notes: 'We can deploy this in 18 months across 4 phases. Phase 1: a 3-month pilot at India Gate and Rashtrapati Bhavan. Phase 2: full rollout to all 500 flags across 8 zones. Phase 3: complete smart system with auto-retract and weather integration. Phase 4: graphene upgrade and scaling to other state capitals. Total investment: 43 crores. Payback: just 8 and a half months.'

slides.title: Closing — Jai Hind
slides.task_brief: Closing slide. Center-aligned. Large tricolour bar at top (taller, 10px). Main text in center: 'Dhvani' in large hero font with saffron 'Dh' and green 'vi'. Below: 'Protecting Our Flag. Optimizing Our Resources.' in white. Three key takeaway badges in a row: '3x Lifespan' (saffron badge), '86.5% Cost Reduction' (green badge), '<3s Alert Speed' (white badge). Bottom: 'Team Hamaraneta | India Innovates Initiative' and 'Jai Hind' in small saffron text. Dark background. Subtle tricolour gradient glow behind the title. Speaker notes: 'Dhvani means sound — the resonant voice of a nation that respects its symbols. Three times the lifespan. 86 percent cost reduction. Damage detected in under 3 seconds. This is not just technology — this is respect for our Tiranga, powered by innovation. Delhi can lead by example, and this system can scale to every state capital in India. Jai Hind. Thank you.'
